modules.name: Introduction to IT & Computer Hardware
modules.description: IT career paths, computing devices, hardware components, system architecture, BIOS/UEFI, and basic hardware troubleshooting.

lessons.title: Overview of IT and Industry Relevance
topics: Introduction to Information Technology
IT career paths and industry demand
Basics of computer systems and digital devices

lessons.title: Identifying Computer Components
topics: Internal components (CPU, RAM, storage, motherboard, PSU, cooling)
External components (input, output, peripheral devices)
Hardware compatibility and performance considerations

lessons.title: Understanding System Architecture
topics: Computer system architecture and data flow
BIOS/UEFI and boot process
Factors affecting system performance

lessons.title: Basic Hardware Troubleshooting
topics: Common hardware failures and symptoms
Troubleshooting tools and techniques (POST, beep codes, Event Viewer)
Best practices for hardware handling and replacement (ESD precautions)

modules.name: Operating Systems & Software Applications
modules.description: Operating system concepts, Windows installation and configuration, software management, and system maintenance and optimization.

lessons.title: Introduction to Operating Systems
topics: What is an operating system
Types of operating systems (Windows, macOS, Linux, mobile)
Open-source vs. proprietary OS

lessons.title: Installing & Configuring Windows OS
topics: Preparing for OS installation (system requirements, boot media)
Installing the Windows OS (partitioning, formatting)
Post-installation configuration (user accounts, drivers, updates)

lessons.title: Working with Software Applications
topics: Types of software (system vs. application, productivity suites)
Software installation and management
File management and cloud storage integration

lessons.title: System Maintenance & Optimization
topics: OS updates and patch management
Performance optimization (Task Manager, disk cleanup, startup apps)
Basic security best practices (permissions, firewalls, antivirus)

modules.name: Networking Fundamentals
modules.description: Network concepts, topologies, protocols, IP addressing, subnetting, network configuration, security, and troubleshooting.

lessons.title: Introduction to Networking Concepts
topics: What is a network (LAN vs. WAN)
Types of network topologies (bus, star, mesh, ring, hybrid)
Networking standards and protocols (TCP/IP, Ethernet, Wi-Fi, DNS, DHCP)

lessons.title: IP Addressing & Network Configuration
topics: Understanding IP addresses (IPv4 vs. IPv6, public vs. private)
Subnetting and network segmentation (subnet masks, CIDR)
Configuring wired and wireless network connections

lessons.title: Basic Network Security & Troubleshooting
topics: Common network security threats
Basic network troubleshooting (ping, tracert, ipconfig)
Maintaining a secure and stable network

modules.name: Security & Cybersecurity Basics
modules.description: Cybersecurity fundamentals, the CIA triad, security measures, encryption, and incident response.

lessons.title: Introduction to Cybersecurity
topics: What is cybersecurity
Common cybersecurity threats (malware, phishing, social engineering)
The CIA Triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability

lessons.title: Implementing Basic Security Measures
topics: User account security (passwords, MFA)
Configuring firewalls and antivirus software
Safe browsing and email security

lessons.title: Cybersecurity Best Practices & Incident Response
topics: Recognizing and responding to security breaches
Introduction to encryption and secure data storage
Maintaining a secure IT environment

modules.name: Troubleshooting & IT Support
modules.description: IT troubleshooting methodologies, diagnosing hardware/software/network issues, documentation, and escalation.

lessons.title: Introduction to IT Troubleshooting
topics: What is troubleshooting and the role of IT support
Troubleshooting methodologies (5-step: Identify, Diagnose, Test, Resolve, Verify)
IT support roles and responsibilities (help desk vs. field technician)

lessons.title: Diagnosing & Resolving Hardware and Software Issues
topics: Common hardware issues and fixes
Software-related issues (crashes, driver conflicts, compatibility)
Using diagnostic tools (Event Viewer, Device Manager, third-party)

lessons.title: Network & Security Troubleshooting
topics: Common network issues and fixes
Diagnosing security issues
Best practices for IT support and documentation

modules.name: Data Storage & Cloud Computing
modules.description: Data storage types, file systems, cloud storage, file management, backup strategies, and disaster recovery.

lessons.title: Introduction to Data Storage
topics: Types of data storage (local vs. cloud, primary vs. secondary)
Storage devices and file systems (HDD, SSD, NVMe, FAT32, NTFS, exFAT)
Data management and organization

lessons.title: Cloud Storage & File Management
topics: Cloud storage providers (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, AWS)
Syncing and sharing files securely
Cloud storage security considerations

lessons.title: Data Backup & Recovery
topics: Backup strategies (full, incremental, differential)
Backup and recovery methods (OS tools, cloud-based solutions)
Disaster recovery planning

modules.name: IT Ethics & Professionalism
modules.description: IT ethics, data privacy laws, intellectual property, software licensing, workplace professionalism, and communication.

lessons.title: IT Ethics & Legal Considerations
topics: Understanding IT ethics
Data privacy and security laws (GDPR, HIPAA, CCPA)
Intellectual property and software licensing

lessons.title: Professionalism & Workplace Communication
topics: IT workplace expectations and professional conduct
Effective communication in IT support and teams
Handling sensitive information and security responsibilities

assessment.title: IT Fundamentals Summative Assessment
items: Hands-on practical exam (50%) — set up Windows workstation, configure networking, implement basic security, troubleshoot system issues
Written knowledge exam (50%) — multiple-choice and scenario-based questions covering hardware, software, networking, cybersecurity, and troubleshooting
